  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client, a Manufacturing (Recycling) company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose


	The Account Officer is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, ensuring compliance with internal controls, tax regulations, and reporting requirements.
	This role supports financial reporting, payroll management, inventory reconciliation, and procurement-related financial documentation, with a strong emphasis on accuracy, timeliness, and regulatory compliance.


Responsibilities
Financial Record-Keeping &amp; Transactions:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all financial transactions.
	Process payments, invoices, and receipts in accordance with financial policies.
	Conduct bank reconciliations and ensure financial records match bank statements.
	Ensure proper documentation for all financial transactions.


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable Management:


	Process vendor payments and ensure all invoices are properly approved and recorded.
	Manage accounts receivable, invoicing clients and following up on outstanding payments.
	Work with procurement to ensure vendor contracts align with financial policies.


Payroll &amp; Employee Benefits Administration:


	Prepare payroll calculations and ensure timely salary disbursement.
	Ensure statutory deductions (PAYE, pensions, NHF, VAT) are processed correctly and remitted.
	Maintain confidential payroll records and ensure compliance with labor regulations.


Tax Compliance &amp; Financial Audits:


	Prepare tax filings and ensure timely payment of all statutory obligations.
	Ensure all financial records are audit-ready and compliant with regulatory standards.


Budgeting &amp; Expense Monitoring:


	Assist in preparing annual budgets and financial forecasts.
	Support in financial planning efforts by providing data-driven financial insights.


Internal Controls &amp; Financial Integrity:


	Implement and enforce financial controls to prevent fraud and mismanagement.
	Conduct regular checks on financial processes to ensure compliance.


Procurement Support &amp; Asset Tracking:


	Process vendor payments upon validation of delivery, documentation, and internal approvals.
	Maintain accurate procurement records and ensure completeness of supporting documents.
	Support inventory tracking, asset tagging, and reconciliation with physical counts.
	Assist in preparing procurement reports and audit schedules.
	Collaborate with the facilities team for joint asset sign-off and deployment logs.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Higher National Diploma degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.
	2&ndash;3 years of experience in internal auditing, preferably in the manufacturing industry
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA is a MUST.


Key Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills.
	Good knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit procedures, and risk management.
	High level of integrity, objectivity, and professionalism.
	Proficiency in audit software/tools and MS Office Suite.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.


Salary
N300,00 - N400,000 Monthly gross.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their current CV to:&nbsp;victor.allu@westfield-consulting.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Account Officer Job at Westfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client, a Manufacturing (Recycling) company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Account Officer is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, ensuring compliance with internal controls, tax regulations, and reporting requirements.
	This role supports financial reporting, payroll management, inventory reconciliation, and procurement-related financial documentation, with a strong emphasis on accuracy, timeliness, and regulatory compliance.


Responsibilities
Financial Record-Keeping & Transactions:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all financial transactions.
	Process payments, invoices, and receipts in accordance with financial policies.
	Conduct bank reconciliations and ensure financial records match bank statements.
	Ensure proper documentation for all financial transactions.


Accounts Payable & Receivable Management:


	Process vendor payments and ensure all invoices are properly approved and recorded.
	Manage accounts receivable, invoicing clients and following up on outstanding payments.
	Work with procurement to ensure vendor contracts align with financial policies.


Payroll & Employee Benefits Administration:


	Prepare payroll calculations and ensure timely salary disbursement.
	Ensure statutory deductions (PAYE, pensions, NHF, VAT) are processed correctly and remitted.
	Maintain confidential payroll records and ensure compliance with labor regulations.


Tax Compliance & Financial Audits:


	Prepare tax filings and ensure timely payment of all statutory obligations.
	Ensure all financial records are audit-ready and compliant with regulatory standards.


Budgeting & Expense Monitoring:


	Assist in preparing annual budgets and financial forecasts.
	Support in financial planning efforts by providing data-driven financial insights.


Internal Controls & Financial Integrity:


	Implement and enforce financial controls to prevent fraud and mismanagement.
	Conduct regular checks on financial processes to ensure compliance.


Procurement Support & Asset Tracking:


	Process vendor payments upon validation of delivery, documentation, and internal approvals.
	Maintain accurate procurement records and ensure completeness of supporting documents.
	Support inventory tracking, asset tagging, and reconciliation with physical counts.
	Assist in preparing procurement reports and audit schedules.
	Collaborate with the facilities team for joint asset sign-off and deployment logs.


Qualifications & Experience


	Higher National Diploma degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.
	2–3 years of experience in internal auditing, preferably in the manufacturing industry
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA is a MUST.


Key Skills & Competencies:


	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills.
	Good knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit procedures, and risk management.
	High level of integrity, objectivity, and professionalism.
	Proficiency in audit software/tools and MS Office Suite.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.


Salary
N300,00 - N400,000 Monthly gross.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their current CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Field Credit Officer Job at Fort Knox Outsourcing




        
    

        
        Fort Knox Outsourcing is poised to serve the growing need of businesses in Nigeria By helping them to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a “Third Party Service Provider” that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Credit Officer

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Follow up on overdue and delinquent loan accounts.
	Conduct field visits to customers, guarantors, and business locations.
	Negotiate repayment plans and settlements where applicable.
	Ensure recovered funds are properly documented and remitted.
	Maintain accurate recovery records and daily recovery reports.
	Track recovery performance against assigned targets.
	Escalate high-risk or chronic default cases to management.
	Educate customers on repayment obligations and consequences of default.
	Handle disputes and repayment challenges professionally.
	Maintain a firm but respectful relationship with customers.
	Ensure recovery activities comply with company policies and regulations.
	Avoid harassment or unethical recovery practices.
	Report suspected fraud or loan misuse promptly.


Requirements


	B.Sc / HND / OND at the minimum.
	2 – 3 years of experience in SME Loan or Merchant Lending
	Basic Knowledge in accounting, finance and mathematics
	Previous work Experience or training in sales (Preferred).


Salary
N170,000 - N175,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualidfied candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and location as the subject of the mail.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Operation Manager",
  "description": "NutriK KFZE, a proud subsidiary of the globally recognized Nutriset Group, plays a vital role in producing and distributing high?quality ready?to?use foods that support the treatment and prevention of malnutrition across Nigeria. Our mission is driven by purpose: to help save lives and improve the well?being of children and families in need. We specialize in the production of essential nutritional products including Ready?to?Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), Ready?to?Use Supplementary Food (RUSF), and Lipid?Based Nutrient Supplements (LNS) which support humanitarian and health interventions nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Operation&nbsp;Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Kano

Overall Mission
General objective:


	The HR Operations Manager is responsible for the efficient day-to-day running of HR operations, ensuring data accuracy and integrity in the HRIS/ERP, managing payroll and benefits administration, and driving regulatory compliance and policy discipline.
	This role also owns the grievance handling process and supports document control and HR policy management to sustain a compliant and well-governed organization.


Responsibilties and Task
Day-to-Day HR Operations:


	Ensure accurate data management within the HRIS/ERP system, including employee records, leave, attendance, and payroll inputs.
	Provide payroll support and coordinate benefits administration in collaboration with finance and external vendors.
	Maintain data integrity, privacy, and security in accordance with company policies and applicable laws.


Compliance Management:


	Monitor and coordinate regulatory filings, registrations, and licenses as required for local and regional operations.
	Prepare for and support internal and external audits, address findings with timely corrective actions.
	Oversee records management, retention schedules, and document control in alignment with legal and regulatory requirements.


Grievance Handling &amp; Employee Relations:


	Act as the process owner for grievance handling; ensure timely, fair, and confidential resolution in line with policy.
	Escalate complex complaints to appropriate stakeholders, coordinate investigations, and track resolution status.
	Promote a respectful work environment and support conflict resolution and employee liaison activities.


Document Control &amp; Policy Management:


	Maintain version-controlled HR policies, standard operating procedures, and related documentation.
	Ensure consistent distribution, training, and acknowledgement of policy updates across the organization.
	Collaborate with HR partners to update processes in line with legal changes and best practices.
	Any other responsibility assigned by line manager.


Key performance indicators


	Accuracy and timeliness of payroll &amp; HR reports
	HR service delivery turnaround time
	Employee onboarding satisfaction rate
	Compliance audit outcomes
	Efficiency improvements in HR processes.


Required Skills
Education Degree:


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Human Resources, Public Administration, Business Administration, or a related field; HR professional certification (e.g., SHRM-SCP, CIPD) preferred.
	Language skills &ndash; English &amp; Hausa is essential.


Work experience:


	5&ndash;8 years of HR operations, administration, and compliance experience, preferably in manufacturing or fast-paced environments.


Technical skills:


	Strong knowledge of local labour laws, employment regulations, and regulatory reporting requirements.
	Experience with HRIS/ERP systems, data integrity, payroll processes, and benefits administration.
	Demonstrated experience in grievance handling, conflict resolution, and employee relations.
	Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and stakeholder management capabilities.


Other required skills:


	Strong organizational skills
	Excellent communication (written and verbal)
	Analytical and data-driven mindset
	Leadership and team management abilities
	Problem-solving and decision-making
	Confidentiality and ethical judgment
	Ability to manage multiple priorities
	Emotional intelligence and interpersonal skills
	Adaptability and change management capabilities.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Operation Manager

Job Location: Kano

Overall Mission
General objective:


	The HR Operations Manager is responsible for the efficient day-to-day running of HR operations, ensuring data accuracy and integrity in the HRIS/ERP, managing payroll and benefits administration, and driving regulatory compliance and policy discipline.
	This role also owns the grievance handling process and supports document control and HR policy management to sustain a compliant and well-governed organization.

	Provide payroll support and coordinate benefits administration in collaboration with finance and external vendors.
	Maintain data integrity, privacy, and security in accordance with company policies and applicable laws.


Compliance Management:


	Monitor and coordinate regulatory filings, registrations, and licenses as required for local and regional operations.
	Prepare for and support internal and external audits, address findings with timely corrective actions.
	Oversee records management, retention schedules, and document control in alignment with legal and regulatory requirements.

	HR service delivery turnaround time
	Employee onboarding satisfaction rate
	Compliance audit outcomes
	Efficiency improvements in HR processes.

Work experience:


	5–8 years of HR operations, administration, and compliance experience, preferably in manufacturing or fast-paced environments.


Technical skills:


	Strong knowledge of local labour laws, employment regulations, and regulatory reporting requirements.
	Experience with HRIS/ERP systems, data integrity, payroll processes, and benefits administration.
	Demonstrated experience in grievance handling, conflict resolution, and employee relations.
	Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and stakeholder management capabilities.


Other required skills:


	Strong organizational skills
	Excellent communication (written and verbal)
	Analytical and data-driven mindset
	Leadership and team management abilities
	Problem-solving and decision-making
	Confidentiality and ethical judgment
	Ability to manage multiple priorities
	Emotional intelligence and interpersonal skills
	Adaptability and change management capabilities.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.

Assistant Digital Producer (Nigeria) Job at BBC Nigeria




        
    

        
        The BBC is an international multimedia broadcaster on radio, TV, online and social networks with a weekly global audience of 320 million. As part of an historic multi-million pound expansion of BBC Africa, the BBC World Service is introducing a wide range of new and exciting language and regional content to serve African and global audiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Digital Producer (Nigeria)

Job Location: Nigeria (Right to work in Nigeria required)
Contract: Fixed-term employment
Duration: Two years
Reports to: Senior Radio Producer

Job Purpose

	The BBC Media Action office in Nigeria is seeking an Assistant Digital Producer to support multimedia production for a multi-component Social and Behaviour Change Communication (SBCC) programme focused on reproductive health and child spacing in Northern Nigeria.
	The Assistant Digital Producer will contribute to the production of radio and digital outputs across genres. Reporting to the Digital Producer, the Assistant Producer Multimedia will be responsible for ideation and creation of impactful radio and digital content that meets BBC quality and editorial standards.
	The Assistant Digital Producer will work with the other members of the production and training department to produce high-quality radio and digital outputs.
	The Assistant Producer will be responsible for ensuring that the produced outputs fulfil the thematic objectives whilst having the greatest possible appeal to the target audience[s].
	The Assistant Digital Producer will contribute to the entire digital production process, from concept development through final product delivery.
	The Assistant Digital Producer will play a key role in planning, conducting and recording interviews, discussions and other content for BBC Media Action’s digital productions.
	The role reports to the Digital Producer and will work closely with other producers.


Main Duties and Responsibilities


	Contribute towards the effective implementation of BBC Media Action’s digital strategy.
	Create social content, including original social videos, multimedia assets, and BBC Media Action editorial content.
	Schedule digital content on BBC Media Action's social media platforms [in Hausa and English/pidgin].
	Posting of social media content on BBC Media Action digital platforms in line with the daily/weekly schedules.
	Work towards achieving the highest levels of traction, reach and engagement possible on BBCMedia Action social media platforms.
	Analyse referral and in-platform metrics and give insight into the behaviour of our social media communities.
	To use all available technical resources to realise digital programme ideas as effectively and as efficiently as possible.
	Work with the digital producer and other members of the Production and Training team to ensure that all digital content meets BBC editorial guidelines.
	Contribute towards making BBC Media Action’s digital outputs as creative and impactful as possible to help us remain a leader in a dynamic and evolving media market.
	Coordinate and conduct research on themes and ensure all digital content information is accurate, clear and unambiguous.
	Perform any other duties that may be assigned.


Required skills, knowledge, and experience


	Sound Editorial judgement and demonstrable understanding of BBC editorial values.
	Experience in producing content for the web or social media.
	Creativity and innovation in the implementation of strategies for individual posts and the management of digital platforms.
	A clear, fluent writing style and an ability to produce strong headlines, summaries and promotional texts in English and Pidgin English. Fluency in written Hausa will be an added advantage.
	A strong visual sense, experience in picture research and image editing skills.
	Video editing skills and fluency in Adobe After Effects or Adobe Premiere Pro.
	Strong attention to detail and an ability to work within established deadlines.
	A self-starter excited by the potential of working in a small, dynamic and growing team.
	Sound knowledge of social media best practices and the social media landscape.
	Sound understanding of BBC Media Action’s audience and the relevant market.
	Demonstrated knowledge or interest in digital trends in media, entertainment, and emerging technologies.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV with a cover letter (in English, PDF or Microsoft Word format) which outlines their experience and suitability for the role, and availability status, by email to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	This role may involve working with groups of vulnerable children, youth, and adults.
	For the successful candidate, mandatory training will be provided on BBC Media Action’s safeguarding policy and staff code of conduct.
	BBC Media Action operates a zero-tolerance policy concerning all forms of abuse and exploitation of vulnerable people.
	Only applicants with a separate cover letter will be considered.
	Please do not send certificates or other attachments.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
	A police background check may form part of the recruitment process.
